rulings.Case Title: HERNAN CARLO MACDULA ET AL VS. SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Of Specialized Loan Servicing, Llc; Meet And Confer Declaration Of Katherine Meleski (Motions)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday May 11, 2022, line 1. DEFENDANT SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Of Specialized Loan Servicing, Llc. Defendant's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is OFF CALENDAR, dismissal on file. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: STRUCTURE PROPERTIES, INC. VS. NICHOLAS CHRISTIE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant'S Attendance At His Deposition, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday May 11, 2022, line 8. PLAINTIFF STRUCTURE PROPERTIES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Defendant'S Attendance At His Deposition, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant. Hearing Required to address whether Defendant will be testifying at trial and any further evidence form Defendant's treating physician under seal. If defendant will not be testifying at trial and the disability is established the motion will be continued to June 3, 2022 for Plaintiff to serve written discovery in lieu of the deposition no later than May 20, 2022. No later than May 27, 2022 Defendant to provide complete verified responses without any boilerplate objections (any objection must be specific to any given request). At the hearing Plaintiff to address whether further responses to discovery will be required, if written responses are defective or insufficient, the Court may order a limited deposition on the areas that are deficient/insufficient. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)
